segments.text:
CELL
Basic unit of ALL living organisms
Bacteria
Protists
Fungi
Plants
Animals

CELL DISCOVERY
Robert Hooke published Micrographia (1665)
Used early microscope to see the units that make up cork
Named these units “cells”
Anton van Leeuwenhoek first to see live cells (1670s)

EARLY CELL BIOLOGY
Theodor Schwann, Matthias Schleiden, and Rudolf Virchow develop first cell theory in mid 1800s
All organisms made of cells
Cell is the smallest living unit
Cells come from other cells (Omnis cellula e cellula)

MODERN CELL BIOLOGY
Keith Porter, George Palade, Albert Claude, and Christian de Duve used cell fractionation and electron microscopy to describe internal structures (1940s-1950s)
Cells are NOT just bags of DNA and proteins

TYPES OF CELLS
Prokaryotes
DNA NOT contained in a nucleus
Includes bacteria and archaea
“small”
Eukaryotes
DNA IS contained in a nucleus
Includes protists, fungi, plants, and animals
“large”

COMPONENTS OF ALL CELLS
Plasma membrane: separates cell from environment
DNA: genetic material
Ribosomes: protein synthesis

EUKARYOTIC CELL COMPONENTS
Nucleus – stores DNA
Endoplasmic reticulum – protein + lipid synthesis
Golgi apparatus – processing + sorting of proteins
Vesicles – movement of materials
Lysosome – degradation of materials
Cytoskeleton – structure and movement
Mitochondrion – cellular respiration
Chloroplast (plants only) – photosynthesis
Peroxisome – breakdown of long chain fatty acids

VIDEO
Inner life of the cell
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Md0bpZcr6o4
